channel.title: Quantum on Entropic Legos
channel.description: Recent content in Quantum on Entropic Legos

item.title: QFT Redux: A Zoomed-Out View of Quantum Field Theory - Part 1
item.author: hugo@example.com (Entropic Legos)
item.description:
TL; DR

Quantum field theory (aka QFT for short) is our best model of three out of the four fundamental forces of the universe. In this post I give a brief overview of the general approach of QFT. It’s primarily intended as a big picture overview of the landscape of QFT for someone who’s already had an intro to the subject. We start with canonical quantisation, but in future episodes we’ll try going into the path integral formalism.

item.title: Lights, Hamilton, Action! - Part 2: A Better Way of Understanding Tunnelling
item.author: hugo@example.com (Entropic Legos)
item.description:
TL; DR

“Our dynamics (including its Einsteinian form) has lagged behind optics; it is still at the stage of geometric optics” - L. de Broglie, in his PhD thesis Research on the Theory of Quanta.

Last time, following the footsteps of Hamilton, we showed that classical mechanics is mathematically equivalent to geometric optics and naturally extended the particle theory of matter to wave theory of matter, for which

$$ \boxed{\lim_{\lambda \rightarrow 0} \text{Wave Mechanics = Classical Mechanics} } $$
